steps.title: Substitute x equals 4/13 into the function
steps.content:
To find $f\left(\frac{4}{13}\right)$, replace $x$ with $\frac{4}{13}$ in the equation $f(x) = x + \frac{9}{13}$:
$$f\left(\frac{4}{13}\right) = \frac{4}{13} + \frac{9}{13}$$

steps.title: Add the fractions with the same denominator
steps.content:
Since both fractions have denominator 13, add the numerators:
$$\frac{4}{13} + \frac{9}{13} = \frac{4+9}{13} = \frac{13}{13} = 1$$
The answer is 1.
